Rename these functions across all Python files in the project at /home/runner/work/erk/erk:
1. `serialize_plan_row` → `serialize_pr_row` (defined in src/erk/tui/data/types.py) 2. `make_plan_row` → `make_pr_row` (defined in BOTH tests/fakes/tests/tui_plan_data_provider.py AND tests/fakes/gateway/plan_data_provider.py) 3. `plan_parsing_ms` → `pr_parsing_ms` (field name in FetchTimings dataclass in src/erk/tui/data/types.py — this is a dataclass field, so rename both the field definition and all attribute accesses like `.plan_parsing_ms`)
Perform the rename in ALL Python files under: - src/erk/ - tests/ - packages/
Make sure to update: 1. Function definitions 2. All import statements 3. All call sites 4. For plan_parsing_ms: the field definition in the FetchTimings dataclass AND all attribute accesses

1→"""Data types for TUI components.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→import dataclasses 6→from dataclasses import dataclass 7→from datetime import datetime 8→from typing import Any 9→ 10→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import IssueFilterState 11→ 12→ 13→@dataclass(frozen=True) 14→class PlanRowData: 15→ """Row data for displaying a plan in the TUI table. 16→ 17→ Contains pre-formatted display strings and raw data needed for actions. 18→ Immutable to ensure table state consistency. 19→ 20→ **Display vs Raw convention:** Many data points have both a raw field (for 21→ logic/predicates) and a display field (for table rendering). Raw fields are 22→ often nullable (None when absent), while display fields are always strings 23→ (dash "-" or empty when absent). Use raw fields in availability predicates 24→ (``ctx.row.pr_number is not None``), display fields for rendering. 25→ 26→ Attributes: 27→ 28→ plan_id: GitHub issue number (e.g., 123). Never None. 29→ plan_url: Full URL to the GitHub issue. None when unavailable. 30→ full_title: Complete untruncated plan title. Empty string possible.
... [269 lines omitted] ...
58→ run_conclusion: Run conclusion ("success", "failure", "cancelled"). 167→ conclusion: Raw run conclusion ("success", "failure", "cancelled"). None if in progress. 219→ warnings: tuple[str, ...] = ()

1→"""Fake plan data provider for testing TUI components.""" 2→ 3→from datetime import UTC, datetime 4→ 5→from erk.tui.data.provider_abc import PlanDataProvider 6→from erk.tui.data.types import FetchTimings, PlanFilters, PlanRowData, RunRowData 7→from erk.tui.sorting.types import BranchActivity 8→ 9→ 10→class FakePlanDataProvider(PlanDataProvider): 11→ """Fake implementation of PlanDataProvider for testing. 12→ 13→ Returns canned data without making any API calls. 14→ Only handles TUI data assembly methods (fetch_plans, etc.). 15→ Domain operations (close_pr, dispatch, etc.) are on FakePrService. 16→ """ 17→ 18→ def __init__( 19→ self, 20→ *, 21→ plans: list[PlanRowData] | None = None, 22→ plans_by_labels: dict[tuple[str, ...], list[PlanRowData]] | None = None, 23→ fetch_error: str | None = None, 24→ ) -> None: 25→ """Initialize with optional canned plan data. 26→ 27→ Args: 28→ plans: List of PlanRowData to return, or None for empty list 29→ plans_by_labels: Per-label-set responses for testing view switching. 30→ When set, fetch_plans() checks this first using the filter labels.
... [384 lines omitted] ...

278→ 279→def serialize_plan_row(row: PrRowData) -> dict[str, Any]: 280→ """Convert PlanRowData to JSON-serializable dict. 281→ 282→ Handles datetime fields (to ISO 8601 strings) and tuple fields 283→ (log_entries, objective_deps_plans) to lists for JSON compatibility. 284→ """ 285→ data = dataclasses.asdict(row) 286→ for key in ("last_local_impl_at", "last_remote_impl_at", "updated_at", "created_at"): 287→ if isinstance(data[key], datetime): 288→ data[key] = data[key].isoformat() 289→ # Convert log_entries tuple of tuples to list of lists 290→ data["log_entries"] = [list(entry) for entry in row.log_entries] 291→ # Convert objective_deps_plans tuple of tuples to list of lists 292→ data["objective_deps_plans"] = [list(entry) for entry in row.objective_deps_plans]
